title: Tune parallelism for throughput and latency
description: Balance operator parallelism, partitioning, and checkpoint interval so the job meets latency without skew or excessive overhead.
body: Use when a job must meet a throughput or end-to-end latency target. Do not raise parallelism blindly — a skewed key or a slow sink leaves added tasks idle while the hot one lags. Actions: measure per-operator backpressure, fix key skew with rebalancing or salting, set parallelism to the bottleneck, and tune the checkpoint interval against its overhead. Validation signals include a flat backpressure profile across tasks and end-to-end latency within target under load. Risk boundary: a too-frequent checkpoint starves processing while too-rare loses more on recovery; if latency and recovery goals conflict, escalate the tradeoff rather than guessing.
